label: Hodgkin disease
quick_take: Hodgkin disease is the textbook "bimodal age-curve" cancer — a young-adult peak plus an older-adult peak — and CI5 captures both peaks cleanly in large US datasets. Incidence is fairly stable globally but several Mediterranean / Eastern European populations show an unusual rise, and Canadian NWT & Yukon women carry a persistent excess.

findings.title: Canada NWT & Yukon women, vol IV: ASR 8.0, 4× the world median
findings.text: The top Hodgkin spatial outlier. Canadian Yukon men also flagged in vol VII at 7.95. Yukon numerators are single-digit in early volumes and carry pop_warning vol VII; treat the magnitude as indicative rather than precise.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Canada NWT & Yukon women, vol IV: ASR 8.0, 4× the world median

findings.title: US Nebraska Black women vol XII: ASR 5.79, 3.5× median
findings.text: Based on 3→15 cases across vols X–XII; the ASR rise is direction-consistent with crude but the absolute numerator is small. Curious because the overall US Black rate is not especially elevated; worth checking the underlying subset.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — US Nebraska Black women vol XII: ASR 5.79, 3.5× median

findings.title: Italian provinces (Ferrara, Benevento, Caserta) and Israel Jews-born-in-Israel women
findings.text: sit at 5–6.5 ASR, 3× median. Italy's persistence of young-adult Hodgkin signals is well-documented.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Italian provinces (Ferrara, Benevento, Caserta) and Israel Jews-born-in-Israel women

findings.title: Italy Latina men +273 %, France Doubs +321 %
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Italy Latina men +273 %, France Doubs +321 %

findings.title: Bimodal age curves in US aggregate data
findings.text: peak at age bands 5 (20–24) and 16+ (75+) — the classic Hodgkin two-peak distribution, with a clear minimum at middle-age. CI5 visualises this fingerprint at scale (18–20 k cases).
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Bimodal age curves in US aggregate data

findings.title: Early-peak at age band 4 (15–19) in Russia St Petersburg, US Detroit, Quebec
findings.text: the "young adult" peak dominant over the older-adult peak.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Early-peak at age band 4 (15–19) in Russia St Petersburg, US Detroit, Quebec

findings.text: Hodgkin vs non-Hodgkin classification has shifted across ICD revisions; some small changes at volume boundaries reflect that.
Pediatric Hodgkin classification changes around ICD-9/10 may affect young-age rates.
The Canadian NWT Hodgkin excess is underreported — a brief Indigenous-health note could be written.
Italy's persistent young-adult Hodgkin signal is worth re-investigating in the context of EBV epidemiology and hygiene-hypothesis data.
Bimodal age curves at vol-XII scale would make a compelling single figure.
